title: Traffic-window and pit-wave field-state features
summary: F1 strategy articles repeatedly emphasize undercut range, clean air, traffic, and Safety Car pit-loss discounts.
rationale: F1 strategy articles repeatedly emphasize undercut range, clean air, traffic, and Safety Car pit-loss discounts. The current best features mostly model tyre-window feasibility, but not whether pitting now creates a usable rejoin window or responds to a field-wide pit wave.
implementation_hint: Within each `Race_Year x LapNumber`, derive features such as cars within undercut range by position band, local field median/dispersion of LapTime_Delta, percentile tyre age, count of nearby rivals already on old tyres, gap-proxy to clean air using neighboring position pace/order statistics, and lap-over-lap spikes in field slowdown to flag Safety Car/VSC-like states.
expected_effect: Adds missing race-context signal for why a theoretically feasible stop happens now rather than 2-3 laps later.
risk: Without true interval gaps, traffic proxies may be noisy; overly aggressive peer aggregates can leak if built with future information instead of same-lap-only context.
